seo.title: Chumphon: Complete Travel Guide 2026 — Gateway to Koh Tao & Gulf Islands
seo.metaDescription: Plan your Chumphon trip with our complete 2026 guide. Ferry to Koh Tao, Thung Wua Laen Beach, diving, seafood markets, and practical tips for Thailand's Gulf coast gateway.

overview: Chumphon sits at the narrow waist of Thailand's peninsula where the Gulf of Thailand begins, and most travelers know it only as the place to catch a ferry to Koh Tao. That's a shame — this low-key fishing province has more to offer than its transit reputation suggests. Wide, white-sand beaches like Hat Thung Wua Laen are nearly empty year-round, an archipelago of more than 40 offshore islands hides world-class dive sites including the famous Chumphon Pinnacle, and the town's fresh seafood markets and fishing quays give you a genuine slice of southern Thai coastal life. For divers, the waters around Chumphon are often less crowded than Koh Tao itself. For backpackers, it's a useful overnight stop between Bangkok and the islands. For independent travelers willing to look beyond the ferry terminal, Chumphon delivers empty beaches, fresh crab, and off-the-beaten-path Thailand at its most unhurried.
highlights: Koh Tao ferry gateway — Lomprayah high-speed catamaran departs 7:00 AM and 1:30 PM daily, 1 hour 45 minutes, 750 THB one-way
Hat Thung Wua Laen — Chumphon's finest beach, 20 km north of town, wide white sand backed by coconut palms with almost no crowds
Chumphon Pinnacle dive site — one of Thailand's top dive sites, a seamount rising to 14 meters with massive schools of fish and frequent whale shark sightings
Mu Ko Chumphon National Park — archipelago of 40+ islands with snorkeling, birdlife, and mangrove trails, entrance 200 THB
Pak Nam Chumphon seafood market — the estuary fishing port where boats bring in the daily catch, fresh crab and squid at local prices
Wat Phra Borommathat Chumphon — a revered temple in the provincial capital with a Srivijaya-era chedi dating back to the 8th century
Khao Matsi Viewpoint — a hilltop panorama over the Chumphon estuary, fishing piers, Hat Pharadon Phap beach and the open Gulf
Hat Pharadon Phap — a quiet 14-km beach south of town favored by kitesurfers and locals, virtually tourist-free
Tham Khao Phlu Hot Spring — natural thermal pools in the jungle, three soaking ponds at around 40C year-round
Fresh seafood at Chumphon Night Market — daily evening market near Kromluang Chumphon Road with grilled seafood, khao man gai, and local sweets

bestTimeToVisit.peak: Nov-Feb: Northeast monsoon keeps the Gulf coast calm and dry. Ideal for beaches, diving, and ferry crossings to Koh Tao. December-January is the coolest and clearest, with temperatures around 27-30C. Ferry services run reliably and dive visibility is excellent at 20-25 meters.
bestTimeToVisit.shoulder: Mar-May: Hot and dry with temperatures peaking at 35C+ in April. Beaches remain good, diving conditions are still excellent, and the Chumphon Pinnacle has some of its best visibility. Fewer tourists than the Gulf island peaks. Songkran (Thai New Year, April 13-15) brings local celebrations.
bestTimeToVisit.lowSeason: Jun-Oct: Southwest monsoon affects the Gulf coast with rain and occasional rough seas. Ferry cancellations are less common here than on the Andaman coast, but happen during severe weather. Beaches are quieter, prices drop 20-30%, and the surrounding hills turn lush green. Inland attractions like the hot spring and Wat Phra Borommathat are unaffected.
bestTimeToVisit.recommendation: Visit November to April for the best combination of beach weather, reliable ferry crossings to Koh Tao, and optimal diving conditions at Chumphon Pinnacle. December to February is ideal. If only passing through to Koh Tao, ferries run year-round with only occasional monsoon cancellations in August-September.

topAttractions.name: Hat Thung Wua Laen Beach
topAttractions.description: Chumphon's best beach, stretching 3 km of wide white sand 20 km north of the town center. Coconut palms line the shore, the water is calm and clear for swimming November to May, and the beach is rarely more than a handful of people — even in peak season. A small promenade of guesthouses, seafood restaurants, and dive shops runs behind the sand. Sunsets here over the Gulf are spectacular.
topAttractions.location: Hat Thung Wua Laen, 20 km north of Chumphon town via Route 3463
tips: Rent a motorbike in town (200-250 THB/day) to reach the beach — songthaews are infrequent
Several beachfront seafood restaurants serve fresh catch straight from the boats — try the grilled squid (pla muek yang) for around 150 THB
Dive operators at the beach offer day trips to Chumphon Pinnacle and the surrounding islands

topAttractions.name: Chumphon Pinnacle Dive Site
topAttractions.description: One of Thailand's top-rated dive sites — an underwater seamount rising from 36 meters to 14 meters below the surface, approximately 23 km off the Chumphon coast. Massive schools of big-eye trevally, barracuda, and batfish swirl around the seamount. Whale sharks and leopard sharks are regularly spotted between March and May. Visibility routinely reaches 20-25 meters in dry season. Reachable on day trips from Hat Thung Wua Laen.
topAttractions.location: 23 km offshore from Hat Thung Wua Laen Beach
topAttractions.entranceFee: 2,500-3,500 THB for a 2-dive day trip from Hat Thung Wua Laen
tips: Advanced Open Water certification recommended — the site has moderate currents
Book with dive operators at Hat Thung Wua Laen beach (PADI certified, multiple operators)
March-May offers the best whale shark encounters and clearest visibility

topAttractions.name: Wat Phra Borommathat Chumphon
topAttractions.description: Chumphon's most revered Buddhist temple, believed to have been founded during the Srivijaya Empire in the 8th century. The temple complex is dominated by a tall, white chedi housing a sacred relic of the Buddha. Located in Sawi district, 45 km south of Chumphon town, it draws pilgrims from across southern Thailand. The surrounding grounds have a peaceful atmosphere and ancient stone inscriptions.
topAttractions.location: Sawi District, 45 km south of Chumphon town on Highway 41
tips: Dress modestly — cover shoulders and knees; sarongs available at the entrance
The temple is busiest during Buddhist holidays and weekends — visit on a weekday morning for tranquility
Combine with a visit to the nearby coastal area of Sawi for local seafood

topAttractions.name: Mu Ko Chumphon National Park
topAttractions.description: A marine national park covering an archipelago of more than 40 islands off the Chumphon coast. The park includes nesting grounds for rare seabirds, coral reefs for snorkeling, and mangrove nature trails. Key islands include Ko Ngam Yai and Ko Ngam Noi (twin islands with beautiful beaches) and Ko Talu (known for its crystal-clear water and colorful marine life). The park is open year-round with peak visibility for diving from November to May.
topAttractions.location: Departs from Pak Nam Chumphon pier, 11 km from Chumphon town
topAttractions.entranceFee: 200 THB national park entrance; boat tours 800-1,500 THB
tips: Day trips to the islands depart from Pak Nam pier — book through hotels or travel agencies in town
Ko Ngam Noi and Ko Ngam Yai are the most accessible islands with the best beaches
Bring snorkel gear or rent from the boat operators — the coral around the islands is in excellent condition

topAttractions.name: Pak Nam Chumphon Seafood Market
topAttractions.description: The beating heart of Chumphon's fishing culture — a riverside fishing port at the Chumphon River mouth, 11 km south of town. Fishing boats return in the morning and evening with fresh Gulf seafood: crab, squid, snapper, prawns, and sea bass. Small seafood restaurants line the quay serving the catch straight from the boats at local prices. The atmosphere — boats unloading, fishermen mending nets, the smell of grilled seafood — is a genuine slice of southern Thai coastal life.
topAttractions.location: Pak Nam Chumphon, 11 km southeast of Chumphon town
tips: Morning (7-9 AM) and late afternoon (4-6 PM) when boats return are the most atmospheric times
Try the steamed blue crab (pu nue) — Chumphon is known throughout Thailand for the quality of its crab
The pier is also the departure point for day trips to the national park islands

topAttractions.name: Khao Matsi Viewpoint
topAttractions.description: A hilltop viewpoint reached by a short drive or walk up a mountain road southeast of Chumphon town. From the summit, a 360-degree panorama takes in the Chumphon Estuary and its fishing piers to the left, the wide expanse of Hat Pharadon Phap beach curving along the Gulf coast, and the open sea dotted with fishing boats. Sunrise and sunset from here are exceptional. The path passes through forest with birdwatching opportunities.
topAttractions.location: Khao Matsi hill, 5 km southeast of Chumphon town center
tips: Accessible by motorbike or on foot (2 km trail from the road)
Go at sunrise or sunset for the best light and cooler temperatures
Binoculars are useful — the panoramic views include coastline in both directions

travelTips: Lomprayah ferry to Koh Tao departs from Chumphon Lomprayah Pier (7 km from town center) at 7:00 AM and 1:30 PM — book tickets online at lomprayah.com or at any travel agency in town for 750 THB
A motorbike rental (200-250 THB/day) from shops near the train station is essential for reaching beaches, viewpoints, and Pak Nam Chumphon — songthaews to outlying areas are scarce
Chumphon is renowned throughout Thailand for its blue crab (pu nue) — order it steamed with a nam jim seafood dipping sauce at Pak Nam quayside restaurants
The train is the best way to arrive from Bangkok — Chumphon train station is in the town center, with overnight sleepers from Hua Lamphong taking 7-8 hours and costing 600-1,200 THB
ATMs are plentiful in Chumphon town (near the market on Sala Daeng Road) but scarce at beaches — withdraw cash before heading to Hat Thung Wua Laen
Ferry to Koh Tao can be combined with onward tickets to Koh Phangan and Koh Samui via Lomprayah or Songserm — buy combo tickets at travel agencies for savings
Chumphon Night Market on Kromluang Chumphon Road runs daily from around 5 PM to 9 PM — the best and cheapest place to eat in town
The Chumphon Pinnacle dive site is less crowded than Koh Tao's main sites — serious divers should consider a day trip from Hat Thung Wua Laen rather than joining the Koh Tao scrum
gettingThere: By train from Bangkok Hua Lamphong: 7-8 hours, from 600 THB (express) or 1,200 THB (overnight sleeper 2nd class) — the most comfortable option. By bus from Bangkok Southern Terminal: 7-8 hours, from 400 THB. By plane: Bangkok Airways operates occasional services via Surat Thani; Chumphon has a small domestic airport 12 km from town. By car from Bangkok: 480 km via Highway 4, approximately 5.5 hours. From Hua Hin: 160 km south on Highway 4, about 2 hours.
gettingAround: Chumphon town itself is walkable near the train station. Songthaews (shared pickup trucks) run set routes within town for 15-30 THB but are infrequent to outlying beaches. Motorbike rental (200-250 THB/day) from shops near the train station is the recommended way to reach Hat Thung Wua Laen (20 km), Pak Nam Chumphon (11 km), and Khao Matsi viewpoint. Tuk-tuks and motorcycle taxis are available in town — negotiate prices before boarding (50-100 THB for short trips). For ferry departures, Lomprayah runs a shuttle bus from their town office to the pier (7 km) for 50 THB.
